   "notes": "AI triage (gemini): Resizing the application window should not cause active remote connections to drop. This indicates a failure in handling UI resize events within the protocol implementations.\nApproach: Ensure that ProtocolBase derived classes (RDP, VNC, Putty) correctly implement ResizeEnd to update their internal controls' dimensions and potentially inform the remote host. Investigate if a missing explicit call to a generic ProtocolBase.Resize() or ProtocolBase.ResizeEnd() is needed within FrmMain.ActivateConnection() or ConnectionTab.FireResizeEnd() for robust handling across all protocols."
 }

   "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The docking framework (WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking) is not correctly handling split panes across multiple monitors, preventing docking on secondary screens, which is a usability bug.\nApproach: Investigate the configuration and usage of the WeifenLuo.WinFormsUI.Docking library within mRemoteNG, focusing on FrmMain.cs and other DockContent implementations, to identify why split panes fail to dock on secondary monitors.  }

 }
diff --git a/.project-roadmap/issues-db/upstream/2119.json b/.project-roadmap/issues-db/upstream/2119.json

   "created_at": "2022-01-12T20:10:50Z",
   "github_updated_at": "2022-01-12T20:13:12Z",
   "body_snippet": "## Expected Behavior\r\nWhen having the option \"File>Options>Startup/Exit>Reconnect to previously opened sessions on startup\" enabled, the opened sessions should be opened again after closing and starting mRemoteNG.\r\n\r\n## Current Behavior\r\nWhen having the option \"File>Options>Startup/Exit>Reconnect to previously opened sessions on startup\" enabled, some opened sessions are forgotten and not opened again after closing and starting mRemoteNG.\r\n\r\nI could observer missing RDP sessions, SSH sessions ar...",
-  "our_status": "new",
-  "priority": null,
+  "our_status": "triaged",
+  "priority": "P2-bug",
   "target_release": null,
   "our_branch": null,
   "our_pr": null,
-  "iterations": [],
+  "iterations": [
+    {
+      "seq": 1,
+      "date": "2026-02-22",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "Connections are not correctly restored on startup because the 'connected' status is conflated with 'should reconnect', and only actively connected sessions are persisted at shutdown, leading to lost sessions."
+    }
+  ],
   "comments": [],
   "comments_cursor": "2022-01-12T20:13:12Z",
   "unread_comments": 0,
   "needs_action": false,
   "waiting_for_us": false,
   "last_synced": "2026-02-15T09:52:50Z",
-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): Connections are not correctly restored on startup because the 'connected' status is conflated with 'should reconnect', and only actively connected sessions are persisted at shutdown, leading to lost sessions.\nApproach: Introduce a new 'AutoConnect' property in ConnectionInfo to explicitly track connections intended for restoration. Update XML serialization/deserialization logic in XmlConnectionNodeSerializer28 and XmlConnectionsDeserializer to persist and load this new property. Modify QuickConnectHistoryLoader and QuickConnectHistorySaver to align with this new persistent 'AutoConnect' flag, ensuring correct 'PleaseConnect' state on application startup. Update PreviousSessionOpener to utilize the new 'AutoConnect' property."
 }

However, if you select the session in the panel and launch it another time, the session will be stablished. So the problem is with the \"Duplicate\" command.\r\n\r\n## Expected Behavior\r\nThe expected behaviour is that the \"Duplicate...",

+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "The ConnectionInfo.CopyFrom method incorrectly copies properties only from the base class, missing properties defined directly in ConnectionInfo, which causes SSH Jump Mode configurations (and other properties) to not be duplicated correctly."

   "comments": [

       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM48kjfO",
@@ -42,5 +49,5 @@

-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The ConnectionInfo.CopyFrom method incorrectly copies properties only from the base class, missing properties defined directly in ConnectionInfo, which causes SSH Jump Mode configurations (and other properties) to not be duplicated correctly.\nApproach: Modify ConnectionInfo.CopyFrom to use this.GetType().GetProperties() instead of GetType().BaseType?.GetProperties(). Add a new test case in ConnectionInfoTests.cs to verify that all properties, including SSH Jump Mode related ones, are correctly copied during duplication."
 }

This significantly improves user experience for setup and migration.\nApproach: Implement a comprehensive 'Export Settings' and 'Import Settings' feature. This feature will involve identifying all user-specific data files (connections, credentials, application settings), packaging them into a single, portable archive (e.g., ZIP) during export, and then extracting and correctly placing them during import. UI elements will be added to the main form to trigger these processes, with appropriate user prompts and security considerations for sensitive data."
 }

This likely stems from changes in ConnectionInfo properties or the associated XmlConnectionNodeSerializer.\nApproach: Investigate ConnectionInfo.cs for any newly added or modified properties that might conflict with existing serialized names (e.g., via XmlElementAttribute or XmlAttributeAttribute). Review XmlConnectionNodeSerializer28.cs to identify any manual serialization logic that could be generating duplicate XML element or attribute names during the serialization process. The fix will likely involve adjusting property names, XML serialization attributes, or the serialization logic to ensure uniqueness."
 }

+        private void mMenToolsExportSettings_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
+        {
+            using var dlg = new SaveFileDialog
+            {
+                Title = "Export Settings",
+                Filter = "mRemoteNG Settings Package (*.zip)|*.zip",
+                FileName = "mRemoteNG-settings.zip",
+                DefaultExt = "zip"
+            };
+
+            if (dlg.ShowDialog(MainForm) != DialogResult.OK)
+                return;
+
+            try
+            {
+                SettingsExporter.ExportSettings(dlg.FileName);
+                MessageBox.Show(
+                    MainForm,
+                    $"Settings exported successfully to:{Environment.NewLine}{dlg.FileName}{Environment.NewLine}{Environment.NewLine}" +
+                    "Copy this file to your new computer and use 'Import Settings...' to restore.",
+                    "Export Settings",
+                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
+                    MessageBoxIcon.Information);
+            }
+            catch (Exception ex)
+            {
+                MessageBox.Show(
+                    MainForm,
+                    $"Failed to export settings:{Environment.NewLine}{ex.Message}",
+                    "Export Settings",
+                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
+                    MessageBoxIcon.Error);
+
+                if (File.Exists(dlg.FileName))
+                {
+                    try { File.Delete(dlg.FileName); } catch { }
+                }
+            }
+        }
+
+        private void mMenToolsImportSettings_Click(object sender, EventArgs e)
+        {
+            using var dlg = new OpenFileDialog
+            {
+                Title = "Import Settings",
+                Filter = "mRemoteNG Settings Package (*.zip)|*.zip",
+                DefaultExt = "zip"
+            };
+
+            if (dlg.ShowDialog(MainForm) != DialogResult.OK)
+                return;
+
+            var confirm = MessageBox.Show(
+                MainForm,
+                "Importing settings will overwrite your current settings files." +
+                $"{Environment.NewLine}mRemoteNG will need to be restarted after import." +
+                $"{Environment.NewLine}{Environment.NewLine}Continue?",
+                "Import Settings",
+                MessageBoxButtons.YesNo,
+                MessageBoxIcon.Warning);
+
+            if (confirm != DialogResult.Yes)
+                return;
+
+            try
+            {
+                SettingsExporter.ImportSettings(dlg.FileName);
+                MessageBox.Show(
+                    MainForm,
+                    "Settings imported successfully. Please restart mRemoteNG to apply the changes.",
+                    "Import Settings",
+                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
+                    MessageBoxIcon.Information);
+            }
+            catch (Exception ex)
+            {
+                MessageBox.Show(
+                    MainForm,
+                    $"Failed to import settings:{Environment.NewLine}{ex.Message}",
+                    "Import Settings",
+                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
+                    MessageBoxIcon.Error);
+            }
+        }
+
         #endregion
     }
 }
